volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: Curculio (Section 5.2.618 - Section 5.2.671c)

sections.label: Section 5.2.618
sections.original: Ego quidem pro istac rem solvi áb trapezita meo .
sections.translation: Why , I paid the money for her through my banker — a sum which I ’ ll have refunded to me fourfold by you and the Procurer .

sections.label: Section 5.2.620
sections.original: Qui scis mercari furtivas atque ingenuas virgines ,
sections.translation: You , who understand how to traffic in young women , kidnapped and of free birth , come you before the judge .

sections.label: Section 5.2.621b
sections.translation: I shan ’ t come .

sections.label: Section 5.2.621c
sections.translation: I may call my witness [*] to the summons .

sections.label: Section 5.2.621d
sections.translation: No , you mayn ’ t .

sections.label: Section 5.2.622
sections.original: Iuppiter te , miles , perdat , intestatus vivito ;
sections.translation: May Jupiter then utterly confound you : live without witnesses [*] then .

sections.label: Section 5.2.623
sections.original: at ego , quem licet , te . accede huc .
sections.translation: But I ’ m a person that he may summon .

sections.label: Section 5.2.623b
sections.translation: What ? A slave summoned as a witness ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.623c
sections.translation: Take you care .

sections.label: Section 5.2.624_625
sections.original: em ut scias me liberum esse . ergo ambula in ius .
sections.translation: Well , that you may know it , I ’ m free .

sections.label: Section 5.2.624_625b
sections.translation: There ’ s for you : take that .

sections.label: Section 5.2.626
sections.translation: O townsmen , townsmen , help .

sections.label: Section 5.2.626b
sections.translation: Why do you bawl so ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.626c
sections.translation: What right have you to strike him ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.627
sections.translation: Because I choose .

sections.label: Section 5.2.627b
sections.original: Accede huc tu , ego illum tibi dedam , tace .
sections.translation: Step this way you ( pushes him towards the CAPTAIN ) ; there , I ’ ll give him up to you . Hold your noise .

sections.label: Section 5.2.628
sections.translation: Phaedromus , prithee do save me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.628b
sections.translation: As I would myself and my own good Genius . Captain , prithee , do tell me whence you got that ring ,

sections.label: Section 5.2.630
sections.translation: which this Parasite purloined from you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.630b
sections.translation: By your knees , I do entreat you to give us that information .

sections.label: Section 5.2.631b
sections.translation: What matters that to you ? You might as well ask after this sabre and this scarf , how each came into my possession .

sections.label: Section 5.2.633
sections.translation: What airs the swaggerer does give himself !

sections.label: Section 5.2.633b
sections.translation: Send that fellow away ( pointing to CURCULIO ) ; then I ’ ll tell you all .

sections.label: Section 5.2.634
sections.translation: It ’ s nonsense what he says .

sections.label: Section 5.2.634b
sections.translation: Prithee , do let me know .

sections.label: Section 5.2.635
sections.original: Ego dícam , surge . hanc rem ágite atque animum advórtite .
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you : ( to PLANESIUM ) get up . Listen to this matter , and give attention . My father Periphanes used to wear it .

sections.label: Section 5.2.637
sections.original: is prius quam moritur mihi dedit tamquam suo ,
sections.translation: He , before he died , gave it to me as being his son , as it was right he should .

sections.label: Section 5.2.638b
sections.translation: O Jupiter !

sections.label: Section 5.2.639
sections.translation: And on that occasion he made me his heir .

sections.label: Section 5.2.639b
sections.translation: O filial affection , do thou preserve me , since I have carefully preserved thee . Welcome , my brother .

sections.label: Section 5.2.641b
sections.translation: How am I to believe that ? Tell me , if you are speaking the truth , who was your mother ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.643
sections.translation: Cleobula .

sections.label: Section 5.2.643b
sections.translation: Who was your nurse ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.643c
sections.translation: Archestrata .

sections.label: Section 5.2.644_645
sections.original: ea me spectatum tulerat per Dionysia .
sections.translation: Amid the sacred rites of Bacchus she had carried me to see the sights ⋯ after we had come thither , when now she had found me a place , a whirlwind arose [*] ; the scaffolding [*] of the stage there tumbled down ; I was greatly alarmed , and then some one , I know not who , seized hold of me trembling and frightened , neither alive nor dead ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.650
sections.original: nec quo me pacto abstulerit possum dicere .
sections.translation: and in what manner he carried me off I cannot say .

sections.label: Section 5.2.651
sections.original: Memini istanc turbam fieri . sed tu dic mihi ,
sections.translation: I recollect that disturbance happening ; but , do you tell me , where is this person , who carried you away ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.652b
sections.translation: I know not ; but ( showing a ring ) this ring I have all along preserved by me , with it long since was I lost .

sections.label: Section 5.2.654b
sections.translation: Give it me to look at .

sections.label: Section 5.2.654c
sections.translation: Are you in your senses to be trusting it to that fellow ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.655b
sections.translation: Do you only leave me alone .

sections.label: Section 5.2.655c
sections.translation: O Jupiter ! This is the same that I presented you upon your birthday ; I recognize it as easily as my own self . Welcome to you , my sister .

sections.label: Section 5.2.658
sections.translation: My brother , welcome to you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.658b
sections.translation: I trust the Gods will prosper this matter for you .

sections.label: Section 5.2.659b
sections.translation: And I , for all of us .

sections.label: Section 5.2.660
sections.original: tu ut hodie adveniens cenam des sororiam ,
sections.translation: Do you , as having this day arrived , give a dinner in honor of your sister he ( pointing to PHAEDROMUS ) , to-morrow , will give one in honor of his marriage ; we promise that .

sections.label: Section 5.2.662
sections.translation: Do you hold your tongue .

sections.label: Section 5.2.662b
sections.original: Non taceo , quando res vortit bene .
sections.translation: I shan ’ t hold my tongue , as things are turning out so well . Captain , do you promise her to him ; I ’ ll give the marriage-portion .

sections.label: Section 5.2.664
sections.translation: What is the marriage-portion ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.664b
sections.original: Egone ? ut semper , dum vivat , me alat .
sections.translation: What , that I give ? Why , that always as long as he lives he ’ s to feed me .

sections.label: Section 5.2.665
sections.translation: I ’ troth , he says what ’ s fair ;

sections.label: Section 5.2.665b
sections.translation: with my consent you shall do so .

sections.label: Section 5.2.666
sections.original: sed leno hic debet nobis triginta minas .
sections.translation: But this Procurer owes me the thirty minae .

sections.label: Section 5.2.667
sections.translation: For what reason so ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.667b
sections.translation: Because he agreed with me on these terms to give it back : that if any one should assert her to be free born in due course of law , he would , without dispute , pay back all the money .

sections.label: Section 5.2.670
sections.translation: Then let ’ s go to the Procurer .

sections.label: Section 5.2.670b
sections.translation: I agree .

sections.label: Section 5.2.670c
sections.translation: This first I wish ; to despatch my matter .

sections.label: Section 5.2.671b
sections.translation: What ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 5.2.671c
sections.translation: That you will betroth her to me .
